rice.name: Rice
rice.description: Rice is a staple food crop grown in flooded fields called paddies. It thrives in warm, humid climates and is one of the most widely consumed grains worldwide.
rice.sowing_method: Transplanting seedlings or direct seeding in flooded fields
rice.watering: Keep fields flooded with 5–10 cm water during growing season
rice.fertilizer: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha. Apply urea in 3 splits
rice.common_pests: Brown planthopper, stem borer, leaf folder — use recommended insecticides
rice.tips: Drain fields 10 days before harvest. Use certified seeds for better yield. Avoid water stress during flowering stage.

maize.name: Maize
maize.description: Maize (corn) is a versatile cereal crop used for food, feed, and industrial purposes. It grows fast and produces high yields under good management.
maize.sowing_method: Direct sowing of seeds 3–5 cm deep in rows
maize.watering: Every 10–12 days. Critical at knee-high, tasseling, and grain fill stages
maize.fertilizer: NPK 150:75:75 kg/ha. Top dress with urea at knee-high stage
maize.common_pests: Fall armyworm, stem borer — spray early with recommended pesticides
maize.tips: Avoid waterlogging. Detassel weak plants for better pollination. Harvest when husks turn brown and dry.

chickpea.name: Chickpea
chickpea.description: Chickpea is a cool-season legume rich in protein. It fixes atmospheric nitrogen and improves soil fertility, making it ideal for crop rotation.
chickpea.watering: 1–2 irrigations only. Sensitive to excess moisture
chickpea.fertilizer: NPK 20:60:40 kg/ha. Rhizobium seed treatment recommended
chickpea.common_pests: Pod borer, aphids — monitor regularly and spray if needed
chickpea.tips: Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture before sowing. Avoid irrigation after pod formation. Harvest when 70% pods turn brown.

kidneybeans.name: Kidney Beans
kidneybeans.description: Kidney beans are protein-rich legumes grown in warm climates. They are highly nutritious and used widely in cooking across the world.
kidneybeans.sowing_method: Direct sowing 3–5 cm deep in well-prepared beds
kidneybeans.watering: Regular watering every 7–10 days. Avoid waterlogging
kidneybeans.common_pests: Bean weevil, whitefly, aphids — use neem-based spray
kidneybeans.tips: Do not overwater during flowering. Support climbing varieties with stakes. Harvest pods when fully dry for seeds.

pigeonpeas.name: Pigeon Peas
pigeonpeas.description: Pigeon pea is a drought-tolerant legume widely grown in tropical regions. It enriches soil with nitrogen and provides food and fodder.
pigeonpeas.soil_type: Well-drained sandy loam to loam, pH 6.0 – 7.5
pigeonpeas.common_pests: Pod borer, pod fly — spray at 50% flowering
pigeonpeas.tips: Intercrop with sorghum or maize for better land use. Avoid waterlogged soils. Harvest in stages as pods mature.

mothbeans.name: Moth Beans
mothbeans.description: Moth bean is an extremely drought-resistant legume native to India. It thrives in arid and semi-arid regions and is rich in protein.
mothbeans.sowing_method: Direct broadcasting or line sowing 2–3 cm deep
mothbeans.watering: Very drought tolerant. Minimal irrigation needed
mothbeans.common_pests: Aphids, whitefly — generally low pest pressure
mothbeans.tips: Ideal for dry land farming. Can be grown on marginal soils. Harvest early morning to prevent pod shattering.

mungbean.name: Mung Bean
mungbean.description: Mung bean is a fast-growing warm-season legume. It is widely used as a vegetable and sprout crop and improves soil fertility.
mungbean.sowing_method: Direct sowing 3–4 cm deep in rows
mungbean.common_pests: Yellow mosaic virus via whitefly — use resistant varieties
mungbean.tips: Short duration crop ideal for intercropping. Harvest in 2–3 pickings. Avoid sowing in waterlogged areas.

blackgram.name: Black Gram
blackgram.description: Black gram (urad dal) is a protein-rich pulse crop widely grown in South Asia. It improves soil nitrogen and is highly nutritious.
blackgram.common_pests: Yellow mosaic virus, thrips — use certified virus-free seeds
blackgram.tips: Use Rhizobium seed treatment for better nitrogen fixation. Harvest when 80% pods turn black. Avoid late sowing.

lentil.name: Lentil
lentil.description: Lentil is a cool-season legume and one of the earliest domesticated crops. It is rich in protein and fiber and widely used in cooking.
lentil.sowing_method: Direct sowing 3–4 cm deep in rows
lentil.soil_type: Well-drained loamy to sandy loam, pH 6.0 – 8.0
lentil.common_pests: Aphids, pod borer — spray at flowering stage
lentil.tips: Rhizobium inoculation improves yield significantly. Harvest early morning to avoid pod shattering. Store in dry conditions.

pomegranate.name: Pomegranate
pomegranate.description: Pomegranate is a drought-tolerant fruit crop grown in arid and semi-arid regions. Its fruits are rich in antioxidants and have high market value.
pomegranate.sowing_season: February – March or July – August (cutting planting)
pomegranate.sowing_method: Stem cuttings or air layering, not seeds
pomegranate.watering: Drip irrigation preferred. Every 3–4 days in summer
pomegranate.fertilizer: NPK 625:250:250 g per plant per year
pomegranate.common_pests: Fruit borer, aphids, mealybug — monitor and spray
pomegranate.tips: Bagging fruits improves quality and reduces pest damage. Prune after harvest for better next season yield. Avoid waterlogging.

banana.name: Banana
banana.description: Banana is a tropical fruit crop that produces year-round. It is one of the most consumed fruits globally and grows well in humid conditions.
banana.watering: Every 3–4 days. Requires high moisture. Drip irrigation ideal
banana.fertilizer: NPK 200:60:300 g per plant. High potassium requirement
banana.common_pests: Banana weevil, Sigatoka leaf spot — regular monitoring needed
banana.tips: Remove excess suckers leaving only one ratoon. Cover bunches with blue polythene bags. Harvest when fingers are plump and rounded.

mango.name: Mango
mango.description: Mango is the king of fruits and a major tropical fruit crop. It has high economic value and can produce fruit for decades once established.
mango.sowing_method: Grafted seedlings preferred over seed sowing
mango.harvest_days: 3 – 5 years for first fruit, harvested May – July
mango.watering: Young plants weekly. Mature trees every 15 days in dry season
mango.fertilizer: NPK 1000:500:1000 g per tree per year for mature trees
mango.common_pests: Mango hopper, fruit fly, powdery mildew — spray at flowering
mango.tips: Withhold irrigation before flowering to induce blooming. Use paclobutrazol for regular bearing. Harvest at mature green stage.

grapes.name: Grapes
grapes.description: Grapes are a high-value fruit crop grown on trellises. They are used for fresh consumption, raisins, juice, and wine production.
grapes.sowing_method: Hard wood cuttings or grafted vines
grapes.harvest_days: First crop in 2nd year, main harvest March – May
grapes.watering: Drip irrigation every 2–3 days. Reduce before harvest
grapes.soil_type: Well-drained sandy loam to loam, pH 6.5 – 7.5
grapes.fertilizer: NPK 600:600:600 g per vine per year
grapes.common_pests: Downy mildew, powdery mildew, thrips — preventive spraying needed
grapes.tips: Pruning is critical — prune twice yearly. Bunch thinning improves berry size. Gibberellic acid treatment increases berry size and loosens bunches.

watermelon.name: Watermelon
watermelon.description: Watermelon is a warm-season fruit crop with high water content. It is widely grown in tropical and subtropical regions for fresh consumption.
watermelon.sowing_method: Direct seed sowing or transplanting seedlings
watermelon.watering: Every 5–7 days. Reduce watering as fruits mature
watermelon.fertilizer: NPK 100:50:100 kg/ha. High potassium for fruit quality
watermelon.common_pests: Aphids, red pumpkin beetle, downy mildew
watermelon.tips: Place fruits on straw to prevent soil contact. Tap fruit — hollow sound indicates ripeness. Stop irrigation 7 days before harvest.

muskmelon.name: Muskmelon
muskmelon.description: Muskmelon is a sweet aromatic fruit crop grown in warm dry climates. It has high demand in summer markets.
muskmelon.sowing_method: Direct seed sowing on raised beds
muskmelon.watering: Every 5–7 days. Stop irrigation 10 days before harvest
muskmelon.common_pests: Aphids, powdery mildew, red pumpkin beetle
muskmelon.tips: Support fruits with nets when grown vertically. Fruit cracks near stem when ripe. Harvest early morning for better shelf life.

apple.name: Apple
apple.description: Apple is a temperate fruit crop requiring cold winters for proper dormancy and fruiting. It is one of the most economically important fruit crops worldwide.
apple.harvest_days: 3 – 5 years for first commercial harvest, August – October
apple.watering: Every 7–10 days in dry periods. Drip irrigation preferred
apple.fertilizer: NPK 700:350:700 g per tree per year
apple.common_pests: Woolly aphid, codling moth, scab — integrated pest management needed
apple.tips: Requires cross-pollination — plant 2 varieties. Thinning fruits improves size and quality. Apply reflective mulch to improve fruit color.

orange.name: Orange
orange.description: Orange is a popular citrus fruit crop grown in subtropical and tropical climates. It is rich in Vitamin C and has high commercial value.
orange.harvest_days: First fruit in 3 – 4 years, harvest November – March
orange.fertilizer: NPK 600:300:600 g per tree per year
orange.common_pests: Citrus psylla, leaf miner, greening disease — monitor regularly
orange.tips: Use disease-free certified budded plants. Foliar spray of micronutrients prevents deficiencies. Harvest when color breaks from green to orange.

papaya.name: Papaya
papaya.description: Papaya is a fast-growing tropical fruit crop that bears fruits within a year of planting. It is rich in vitamins and widely used fresh and processed.
papaya.sowing_method: Nursery raised seedlings transplanted at 45 days
papaya.watering: Every 5–7 days. Highly sensitive to waterlogging
papaya.fertilizer: NPK 250:250:500 g per plant per year
papaya.common_pests: Papaya ringspot virus via aphids, mealybug — use virus-free seeds
papaya.tips: Plant 10% male plants for pollination. Avoid transplanting in waterlogged areas. Harvest when skin shows yellow streaks.

coconut.name: Coconut
coconut.description: Coconut is a versatile tropical palm crop used for food, oil, fiber, and timber. It is called the tree of life due to its multiple uses.
coconut.sowing_method: Mature seed nuts planted in pits
coconut.harvest_days: First nuts in 6 – 8 years, harvests every 45 days
coconut.watering: Every 5–7 days. Basin irrigation or drip
coconut.fertilizer: NPK 500:320:1200 g per palm per year
coconut.common_pests: Rhinoceros beetle, red palm weevil, eriophyid mite
coconut.tips: Fill crown with sand during summer to prevent rhinoceros beetle. Apply organic mulch in basin. Intercrop with banana or turmeric for additional income.

cotton.name: Cotton
cotton.description: Cotton is a major fiber crop grown in tropical and subtropical regions. Bt cotton varieties dominate modern cultivation due to pest resistance.
cotton.sowing_method: Direct seed sowing after acid delinting
cotton.watering: Every 10–15 days. Critical at flowering and boll development
cotton.soil_type: Deep black cotton soil or loamy soil, pH 6.0 – 8.0
cotton.common_pests: Bollworm, whitefly, thrips — Bt varieties have bollworm resistance
cotton.tips: Use Bt hybrid seeds for bollworm resistance. Harvest in dry weather only. Pick cotton in 3–4 pickings as bolls open.

jute.name: Jute
jute.description: Jute is a natural fiber crop grown in humid tropical regions. It is biodegradable and widely used for packaging, textiles, and geotextiles.
jute.watering: Requires adequate rainfall 150–200 cm. Supplemental irrigation if needed
jute.common_pests: Hairy caterpillar, stem rot, semilooper — monitor from early stage
jute.spacing: Broadcast sown, thin to 5–7 cm apart
jute.tips: Harvest at 50% flowering stage for best fiber quality. Ret (soak) stalks in water 15–20 days for fiber extraction. Early sowing gives better yield.

coffee.name: Coffee
coffee.description: Coffee is a high-value plantation crop grown under shade in tropical highlands. Arabica and Robusta are the two main commercial species.
coffee.sowing_method: Nursery raised seedlings transplanted at 9 – 12 months
coffee.harvest_days: 3 – 4 years for first harvest, November – February
coffee.watering: Every 7–10 days in dry season. Shade trees help retain moisture
coffee.soil_type: Deep well-drained loamy soil rich in organic matter, pH 6.0 – 6.5
coffee.common_pests: Coffee berry borer, white stem borer, leaf rust — integrated management
coffee.tips: Plant shade trees like silver oak before coffee planting. Harvest only fully red ripe cherries for best quality. Pulping and drying within 24 hours of harvest is critical.

adzukibeans.name: Adzuki Beans
adzukibeans.description: Adzuki bean is a small red legume widely grown in East and South Asia. It is rich in protein, fiber, and minerals and commonly used in sweet pastes, desserts, and curries.
adzukibeans.sowing_method: Direct seed sowing 3–4 cm deep in rows
adzukibeans.soil_type: Well-drained loamy to sandy loam, pH 5.5 – 7.0
adzukibeans.fertilizer: NPK 20:40:20 kg/ha at sowing. Rhizobium inoculation recommended
adzukibeans.common_pests: Bean weevil, aphids, pod borer — monitor from flowering stage
adzukibeans.tips: Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture before sowing for better nitrogen fixation. Harvest when pods turn straw-yellow. Store in cool dry conditions to prevent weevil damage.

rubber.name: Rubber
rubber.description: Rubber (Hevea brasiliensis) is a tropical plantation crop grown for natural latex used in tyre, glove, and industrial product manufacturing. It is a long-term perennial crop with high economic returns.
rubber.sowing_method: Budded stumps or polybag plants transplanted in pits
rubber.harvest_days: Tapping begins at 5 – 7 years; productive for 30+ years
rubber.watering: Rainfall-dependent. Requires 200+ cm annual rainfall. Supplemental irrigation in dry months
rubber.soil_type: Deep well-drained laterite or loamy soil, pH 4.5 – 6.0
rubber.fertilizer: NPK 10:10:10 kg per tree annually; split into 2–3 doses
rubber.common_pests: Abnormal leaf fall (Phytophthora), pink disease, scale insects — preventive fungicide sprays needed
rubber.yield: 1500 – 2500 kg dry rubber per hectare per year
rubber.tips: Tapping should be done early morning before sunrise. Use proper tapping knives and avoid cutting too deep. Stimulants like Ethephon can be used to increase latex yield. Rest trees during dry season.

sugarcane.name: Sugarcane
sugarcane.description: Sugarcane is a tall tropical grass crop grown for sugar, jaggery, and ethanol production. It is one of the most productive crops in terms of biomass and is a major source of the world's sugar supply.
sugarcane.sowing_method: Sett (stem cutting) planting with 2–3 buds per sett, in furrows
sugarcane.watering: Every 7–10 days. Critical at germination, tillering, and grand growth stages. Drip irrigation saves 30–40% water
sugarcane.soil_type: Deep well-drained loamy or clay loam, pH 6.0 – 7.5
sugarcane.fertilizer: NPK 250:85:115 kg/ha. Apply nitrogen in 3–4 splits
sugarcane.common_pests: Top shoot borer, internode borer, red rot disease, smut — treat setts before planting
sugarcane.spacing: 90 cm between rows, setts end-to-end in furrow
sugarcane.tips: Treat setts with hot water (52°C for 30 minutes) to eliminate ratoon stunting disease. Trash mulching conserves moisture and reduces weeds. Harvest at optimum maturity (high sucrose content) for best sugar recovery.

tobacco.name: Tobacco
tobacco.description: Tobacco is a commercial crop grown for its leaves which are processed for cigarettes, cigars, and other tobacco products. It is a high-value cash crop requiring precise management.
tobacco.sowing_method: Nursery seedlings raised and transplanted at 6–8 weeks
tobacco.watering: Every 7–10 days. Avoid excess irrigation after topping
tobacco.fertilizer: NPK 90:60:120 kg/ha. Avoid excess nitrogen for quality leaf
tobacco.common_pests: Tobacco caterpillar, aphids, mosaic virus — use virus-free seedlings
tobacco.tips: Top the plant (remove flower buds) to redirect energy to leaf growth. Remove suckers after topping regularly. Harvest leaves progressively from bottom to top as they mature. Curing method affects leaf quality significantly.

tea.name: Tea
tea.description: Tea (Camellia sinensis) is a perennial plantation crop grown in tropical and subtropical highlands. It is one of the most widely consumed beverages globally and a major export commodity.
tea.germination_days: Cuttings root in 3 – 4 months in nursery
tea.harvest_days: First plucking at 3 – 4 years; productive for 50+ years
tea.watering: Requires 150–250 cm annual rainfall, well distributed. Irrigation in dry months every 7–10 days
tea.sunlight: Partial to full sun. Shade trees beneficial in hot climates
tea.fertilizer: NPK 60:30:30 kg/ha per year. Split into 3–4 doses
tea.common_pests: Tea mosquito bug, red spider mite, blister blight — integrated pest management essential
tea.yield: 2000 – 4000 kg made tea per hectare per year
tea.tips: Pluck only the top 2 leaves and a bud (two-and-a-bud standard) for best quality. Maintain plucking round of 7–12 days. Skiffing and pruning every 3–5 years rejuvenates the bush. Shade regulation improves flavor profile.
